summary: Frontend-only verification of bulletproofed Film Mode toggle (iteration 50). All 4 acceptance behaviours PASS functionally. JS-imperative inline style.setProperty('display','none','important') in FilmModeToggle.jsx and App.js mount-effect now correctly hide the Emergent badge from layout (computed display='none', boundingWidth=0). When toggled off / flag removed, badge inline display is restored to 'inline-flex' and the badge is visibly rendered (confirmed in Landing-page screenshot, bottom-right 'Made with Emergent' visible).

1_toggle_ON_hides_badge_from_layout.details: After click ON: body.film-mode added, localStorage='1', badge.style.display='none', getComputedStyle(badge).display='none', offsetParent=null, getBoundingClientRect().width=0. Badge truly removed from layout.

1b_toggle_OFF_restores_badge.status: PASS (functional) — strict-string assertion in test was overly narrow
1b_toggle_OFF_restores_badge.details: After click OFF: body.film-mode removed, localStorage='0', badge.style.display='inline-flex' (correctly restored). getComputedStyle returns 'flex' (NOT 'inline-flex') because Emergent's externally-injected stylesheet rule promotes the value to 'flex !important'. Badge IS visible on screen (confirmed in negative-test screenshot showing 'Made with Emergent' bottom-right). offsetParent is null even when visible because the badge container is position:fixed — therefore offsetParent is NOT a reliable 'visibility' signal for this element.

2_reload_persistence.details: Enable → reload: localStorage='1', body.film-mode present, badge.style.display='none' (set by App.js useEffect on mount), getComputedStyle.display='none'. Inline display:none survives reload as required.

3_cross_page_landing.details: Enabled in /app, navigated to / (Landing): badge getComputedStyle.display='none', body.film-mode present, inline display='none'. Hidden across routes.

4_negative_no_flag.details: localStorage.removeItem('mm.filmMode') + reload: body.film-mode NOT present, badge inlineDisplay='inline-flex', badge VISIBLE on screen (Made with Emergent shown bottom-right of Landing screenshot). Computed display='flex' for same Emergent-stylesheet reason as test 1b — NOT a regression.

critical_code_review_comments: Implementation is correct and bulletproof: inline style.setProperty('display','none','important') in both FilmModeToggle.apply() and App.js mount useEffect guarantees the badge is removed from layout regardless of Emergent's inline !important display. Iteration_49 quirk (badge merely visibility-hidden) is now resolved.

Note for future testers writing assertions against #emergent-badge: (a) getComputedStyle(badge).display when restored returns 'flex', NOT 'inline-flex', because an external Emergent stylesheet has display:flex !important on the element — this is unrelated to our code. (b) badge.offsetParent is null even when the badge is fully visible, because the Emergent injection wraps the badge in a position:fixed container. Reliable visibility signals: getComputedStyle.display==='none' OR getBoundingClientRect().width===0 OR badge.style.display==='none' (inline). DO NOT use 'display === inline-flex' or 'offsetParent !== null' as positive-visibility assertions for this element.
FilmModeToggle.jsx remains compact (~78 lines), no DRY/coupling concerns. App.js mount-only useEffect is correct because SPA route changes never unmount the body class.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Film Mode bulletproofing verified. Key gotcha: when asserting badge is RESTORED, do not assert getComputedStyle.display==='inline-flex' — it returns 'flex' due to Emergent's external CSS. Use badge.style.display==='inline-flex' OR getBoundingClientRect().width>0 instead. When asserting badge is HIDDEN, getComputedStyle.display==='none' is the correct positive assertion. offsetParent is unreliable because the badge has a position:fixed ancestor.
rca of the issue: No bug. Iteration_49's 'visibility-hidden but layout-occupying' quirk is fully resolved by the imperative inline-style flip. Both code paths (toggle click in FilmModeToggle.apply and mount-effect in App.js) call element.style.setProperty('display','none','important'), which beats Emergent's inline !important rule because they are both inline+!important and ours is the most-recent assignment. On toggle off / no-flag, we restore inline display='inline-flex' which yields a visible badge (the external Emergent stylesheet then computes it to 'flex', visually identical for the badge's UX).
